title: Three-layer knowledge architecture
description: Raw sources (immutable) -> Wiki (LLM-owned markdown) -> Schema (CLAUDE.md config). Clean separation of concerns where human curates sources and schema, LLM maintains the wiki.

title: Bookkeeping is the bottleneck, not thinking
description: Humans abandon wikis because maintenance burden (cross-references, summaries, contradictions) grows faster than value. LLMs eliminate this by making maintenance cost near zero.

title: Ingest-Query-Lint operation triad
description: Three core operations: Ingest (process source into wiki, touch 10-15 pages), Query (search wiki + synthesize answer, file good answers back), Lint (health-check for contradictions, orphans, stale claims, missing cross-refs).

title: Index + Log dual navigation
description: index.md for content-oriented navigation (catalog with links, summaries, metadata). log.md for chronological record (append-only, parseable prefixes). Avoids need for embedding-based RAG at moderate scale.

title: qmd — local markdown search engine
description: BM25/vector hybrid search with LLM re-ranking, on-device. Has CLI and MCP server interfaces. Useful when wiki outgrows index-file navigation.

title: Schema as the key differentiator
description: The schema document (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) is what makes the LLM a disciplined wiki maintainer vs generic chatbot. Co-evolved between human and LLM over time.

title: Answers compound into wiki
description: Good query answers should be filed back as new wiki pages. Explorations compound in the knowledge base alongside ingested sources. This prevents knowledge loss to chat history.

title: Vannevar Bush Memex connection
description: The LLM Wiki pattern is a modern realization of Bush's 1945 Memex vision — personal, curated knowledge store with associative trails. The missing piece was maintenance, which LLMs now handle.

title: No scaling analysis beyond moderate scale
description: Claims index.md works at ~100 sources / hundreds of pages. No discussion of what happens at 1000+ sources, fragmentation, or when contradictions accumulate faster than lint can resolve them.

title: No multi-session continuity discussion
description: The gist assumes single-session or manual session management. No treatment of how to maintain wiki consistency across LLM sessions with context resets, compaction, or tool failures.
